title: Health-related messages for all tobacco product packaging: Health information messages - Canada.ca
content:
## Health-related messages for all tobacco product packaging: Health information messages
- Health Warnings on Packages
- Health Information Messages
- Toxicity Information
- Health Warnings on Products
- Series 1: Health-related messages for all tobacco product packaging Cigarettes, little cigars, and cigarette tobacco
- Cigarettes, little cigars, and cigarette tobacco
- Series 2: Health-related messages for all tobacco product packaging Cigarettes, little cigars, and cigarette tobacco
- Cigarettes, little cigars, and cigarette tobacco
The Tobacco Products Appearance, Packaging and Labelling Regulations (TPAPLR) mandate health-related messages for all tobacco products, as follows:
- Health warnings on packages covering at least 75% of the primary display area of most tobacco product packages
- Health warnings on cigarettes, little cigars with tipping paper, and tubes; health warnings extending from one end of the product on cigarettes without tipping paper
- Health information messages on the inside of cigarettes, little cigars, and cigarette tobacco packages
- Toxicity information on the exterior surface of all packages
The TPAPLR also include a rotation scheme that alternates all health-related messages every 24 to 36 months depending on the product. The health-related messages for each series are provided here as reference.
Note: Health Canada is the copyright owner of all health-related messages except for those marked with an asterisk (*). Reproducing any of these images without prior permission is prohibited. Permission to use the images owned by Health Canada can be requested by submitting this form to pregs@hc-sc.gc.ca . For those images whose copyright is not owned by Health Canada, i.e. those marked with an asterisk (*), please contact pregs@hc-sc.gc.ca to obtain the owner's contact information.

## Series 1: Health-related messages for all tobacco product packaging

### Health Information Messages – Cigarettes, little cigars, and cigarette tobacco
Find your reasons to quit smoking!
Be inspired to quit for good!
gosmokefree.gc.ca/quit 1-866-366-3667
"Food tastes better and my sense of smell is back. I have a lot more energy and I am a lot more productive."
Jennifer Smoked for 20 years Quit at age 36.
Cigarettes are highly addictive.
gosmokefree.gc.ca/quit 1-866-366-3667
Quitting cigarettes improves your health and your chances of having a healthy baby.
Find support to help you quit.
Talk to a health care provider. They can help you find a way that works for you.
gosmokefree.gc.ca/quit 1-866-366-3667
Toxic chemicals in tobacco smoke can harm your skin.
When you quit, more oxygen gets to your skin. This makes it healthier and protects it from premature aging.
gosmokefree.gc.ca/quit 1-866-366-3667
Visible effects facts
- Live a longer, healthier life
gosmokefree.gc.ca/quit 1-866-366-3667
Short sessions of physical activity can help you cut down the urge to smoke.
Quitting cigarettes improves lung health.
gosmokefree.gc.ca/quit 1-866-366-3667
A person who smokes daily can save more than $200 a month when they quit.
The savings can help ease stress and anxiety.
Improve your physical and mental health by quitting.
gosmokefree.gc.ca/quit 1-866-366-3667
The human papillomavirus (HPV) is the main cause of cervical cancer.
Cigarettes weaken the immune system and make it harder to fight off an HPV infection.
gosmokefree.gc.ca/quit 1-866-366-3667
Cervical cancer facts
Quitting cigarettes reduces your risk of heart disease and heart attack.
Within minutes, your heart rate drops to normal.
Within days, your blood pressure begins to drop and circulation improves.
gosmokefree.gc.ca/quit 1-866-366-3667
Improve your chances of quitting successfully:
- Talk to a healthcare provider about quitting
- Call the quit line for advice
- Consider using nicotine replacement therapy
Find a way that works for you!
gosmokefree.gc.ca/quit 1-866-366-3667
Protect your loved ones and be a positive role model.
Support your partner by quitting cigarettes.
Smoke-free homes are healthier for children.
gosmokefree.gc.ca/quit 1-866-366-3667
Second-hand Smoke and Pregnancy facts
- Live a longer, healthier life
gosmokefree.gc.ca/quit 1-866-366-3667
Quitting Cigarettes Makes Breathing Easier.
It reduces your risk of lung cancer and lung disease.
Physical activities, like walking and exercising, get easier.
gosmokefree.gc.ca/quit 1-866-366-3667

## Series 2: Health-related messages for all tobacco product packaging

### Health Information Messages – Cigarettes, little cigars, and cigarette tobacco
- Reduce your risk of having a heart attack or stroke
- Live a longer, healthier life
gosmokefree.gc.ca/quit 1-866-366-3667
- Reduce your risk of heart disease and stroke
- Make breathing easier
About 8 million Canadians have improved their health by quitting cigarettes. You can too!
gosmokefree.gc.ca/quit 1-866-366-3667
When you quit, your body has to adjust. Withdrawal feelings will pass. To cope:
Get more active. It will help you overcome nicotine cravings and improve your wellbeing.
Drink water, eat well, and get enough sleep.
Nicotine is the drug in tobacco that causes addiction.
gosmokefree.gc.ca/quit 1-866-366-3667
Toxic chemicals in tobacco smoke can harm your skin.
When you quit, more oxygen gets to your skin. This makes it healthier and protects it from premature aging.
gosmokefree.gc.ca/quit 1-866-366-3667
Visible effects facts
You can reduce your risk of:
- Irregular menstruation
Become a smoke-free woman today!
gosmokefree.gc.ca/quit 1-866-366-3667
Cervical cancer facts
Menstruation and Menopause facts
Here are some benefits to quitting cigarettes:
- Improved health for you during pregnancy
- Lower risk of preterm birth
- Healthier start in life for your baby
gosmokefree.gc.ca/quit 1-866-366-3667
Second-hand Smoke and Pregnancy facts
Most people try several times before quitting for good.
- Seek social support for your efforts.
- Consider using nicotine replacement therapy.
Quitting smoking is the most important thing you can do to improve your health.
gosmokefree.gc.ca/quit 1-866-366-3667
People who smoke are about 30% more likely to develop type 2 diabetes.
If you have diabetes, quitting will help you manage your blood sugar levels.
gosmokefree.gc.ca/quit 1-866-366-3667
Quitting will reduce your risk of lung cancer and lung disease.
Within weeks, your lungs begin to heal and breathing gets easier.
Within months, you will cough and wheeze less.
gosmokefree.gc.ca/quit 1-866-366-3667
Getting support makes quitting easier.
- Talk to a healthcare provider about quitting
- Call the quit line for advice
- Consider using nicotine replacement therapy
You don't have to do it alone!
gosmokefree.gc.ca/quit 1-866-366-3667
It can reduce your anxiety and help you gain control of your life.
Try getting more active. Exercise can help you manage withdrawals and increase your wellbeing.
Be free of your tobacco addiction!
gosmokefree.gc.ca/quit 1-866-366-3667
- Live a longer, healthier life
gosmokefree.gc.ca/quit 1-866-366-3667
Quitting cigarettes improves your blood circulation.
Don't let cigarettes keep you down!
gosmokefree.gc.ca/quit 1-866-366-3667
